items.rationale: The exact acute-otitis-externa symptom section supports itch, pain, discharge, onset, associated features, relevant negatives, modifying factors, general appearance, vital signs, and ENT examination without diagnosing infection.

items.final_wording: Supports distinguishing AOE from other otalgia or otorrhoea, pain assessment, clinician-selected topical management, administration discussion, and reassessment after non-response.

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports distinguishing AOE from other otalgia or otorrhoea, pain assessment, clinician-selected topical management, administration discussion, and reassessment after non-response.

items.rationale: The exact key-action section supports clinician concern, entered plan, safety net, and follow-up only when documented; it does not prescribe drops, antibiotics, wick placement, or referral.

source.title: Updated Clinical Practice Guideline: Acute Otitis Externa — Official Fact Sheet and Key Action Statements
source.population: People aged 2 years or older with diffuse acute otitis externa.
source.setting: Primary, emergency, paediatric, internal-medicine, and otolaryngology settings.
source.exclusions: Exact for diffuse acute otitis externa symptoms, modifiers, and key actions; partial for broad ear pain and not validated for UAE pathways.

exact_location.heading: Fact sheet — AOE symptoms and modifying factors

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports swimmer’s-ear terminology, water exposure, ear-canal inflammation, itching, fullness, hearing change, external-ear pain or tenderness, diabetes, immunocompromise, and tympanic-membrane context.

added_items.rationale: The exact acute-otitis-externa symptom section supports itch, pain, discharge, onset, associated features, relevant negatives, modifying factors, general appearance, vital signs, and ENT examination without diagnosing infection.

added_items.final_wording: Supports distinguishing AOE from other otalgia or otorrhoea, pain assessment, clinician-selected topical management, administration discussion, and reassessment after non-response.

source.title: Updated Clinical Practice Guideline: Acute Otitis Externa — Official Fact Sheet and Key Action Statements
source.population: People aged 2 years or older with diffuse acute otitis externa.

source.exclusions: Exact for diffuse acute otitis externa symptoms, modifiers, and key actions; partial for broad ear pain and not validated for UAE pathways.

exact_location.heading: Fact sheet — significant guideline action statements

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports distinguishing AOE from other otalgia or otorrhoea, pain assessment, clinician-selected topical management, administration discussion, and reassessment after non-response.

added_items.rationale: The exact key-action section supports clinician concern, entered plan, safety net, and follow-up only when documented; it does not prescribe drops, antibiotics, wick placement, or referral.
